summaries.1: Chapter I defines the Data Act’s subject matter, scope, and key definitions — including product data, related service data, users, data holders, and the relationship with the GDPR where personal data is involved.
summaries.2: Chapter II sets rules for business-to-consumer and business-to-business sharing: making product and service data accessible to users, user rights to share with third parties, and related obligations.
summaries.3: Chapter III governs data holders that must make data available under other Union law — conditions, compensation, dispute settlement, and technical protection measures.
summaries.4: Chapter IV addresses unfair contractual terms imposed on enterprises regarding data access and use, with particular protection for SMEs.
summaries.5: Chapter V allows public sector bodies and certain Union bodies to request data in situations of exceptional need, with safeguards on purpose limitation and compensation.
summaries.6: Chapter VI removes obstacles to switching between data processing services (including cloud), with contractual transparency, gradual removal of switching charges, and technical switching requirements.
summaries.7: Chapter VII restricts unlawful international governmental access to and transfer of non-personal data held in the EU.
summaries.8: Chapter VIII establishes interoperability requirements for data, data sharing mechanisms, data spaces, and smart contracts used in data sharing agreements.
summaries.9: Chapter IX covers competent authorities, complaints, remedies, penalties, model contractual terms, and the role of the European Data Innovation Board (EDIB).
summaries.10: Chapter X limits the sui generis database right under Directive 96/9/EC for certain databases containing data made available under the Data Act.
summaries.11: Chapter XI contains final provisions on relationships with other Union acts, delegated powers, committee procedure, evaluation, and dates of application (including 12 September 2025).
